According to the attached history document from 2026-06-28, Turn 106 is another attachment-only continuation. The three required current-turn attachments are present, and I did not use File Library material.

Turn 106 ‚ÄĒ attachment-only idempotence remains closed

Since rS and rS‚ąí1 generate the same closed normal subgroup of F, this identifies the two closed normal relator subgroups and descends toőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The descended isomorphism satisfies exactly the required conditionŌÄH‚ąėő¶=ŌÄS.This is the original over-G isomorphism asked for in the problem statement.

The frozen proof chain is still:H1 -Nielsen¬†equivalence+¬†residual¬†restricted-Lie¬†induction+¬†normalized¬†Koszul‚ÄďWitt¬†coefficient¬†induction+D.1‚ÄďD.4+¬†finite¬†filtered¬†Nielsen¬†compactness‚üĻőďS‚ČÖGőďH.The compactness mechanism remains the global one: finite filtered Nielsen/cocycle data SN,J are nonempty at every coefficient-and-degree truncation; their compatible inverse limit gives a continuous over-G automorphism ő¶:F‚ÜíF with ő¶(rS‚ąí1)=rH. The quotient descent then gives őďS‚ČÖGőďH.The measured-certificate coordinate also remains unchanged. The current attachments are only the prompt, problem statement, and history ledger. They do not include genuine object-level nonlinear Certificate D artifacts: no measured D.1‚ÄďD.4 scripts, matrices, pivot tables, or measured-output logs are attached. Therefore the strict Certificate D gate remains

NO_MEASURED_INPUT,not PASS, not FAIL, and not REJECTED_AS_LEDGER_DERIVED. The history may record verifier claims as theorem-ledger evidence, but it is not itself newly attached measured nonlinear Certificate D input.

So no Magnus tower, coefficient tower, degree-16 case, A2 -layer, t-layer, or Certificate D subcase should be reopened under the current attachment-only input. The invariant state is:history/problem/prompt¬†only‚üĻ(őďS‚ČÖGőďH,¬†NO_MEASURED_INPUT).
